events.title: Ethics in DSM Practice - March 20 2026 - Rasmuson Hall
events.description: Continuing Education: 3 Credit Hours Available. See training for more details. Vanessa Meade, PsyD, LCSW (she/her), is an assistant professor at the University of Alaska Anchorage's School of Social Work. She is also a U.S. Army/Gulf War Veteran and a former Alaska State Trooper. Dr. Meade is a licensed clinical social worker and psychologist in Alaska. She has also worked as a clinician/therapist in rural community mental health agencies and juvenile justice. She is a certified mindfulness teacher and facilitates mindfulness-based programs at UAA with students, faculty, and staff, as well as with military veterans and wildland firefighters through online and multi-day residential retreat programs throughout the United States. She serves on several state and national advisory committees and boards. Dr. Meade also focuses on equity and social justice in her work, including within the LGBTQ+ community. View the CACHE Refund Policy here.

events.title: CALS 2026
events.description: Comprehensive Advanced Life SupportThis course consists of interactive sessions in cardiac, traumatic, pediatric, obstetrical, neonatal and medical advanced life support. Course materials establish the CALS Universal Approach to critically ill or injured patients, which defines the manner by which care providers evaluate and treat emergency patients.The CALS Course was designed to provide physicians, registered nurses, physician assistants, nurse practitioners and paramedics with theopportunity to learn and practice infrequently used lifesaving skills. Participants are encouraged to attend in teams, and courses are limited to 24 participants per session. All applications will be evaluated for admission to the course. Priority will be given to rural providers.Skills Taught Include:The CALS

events.title: ACFTA Live Sessions 2025
events.description: Each victim of violence deserves to be treated with dignity and respect, and to have evidence of the crimes committed against them preserved for future prosecution. Unfortunately, many areas of the state lack the ability to document and collect medical evidence to help protect victims of crime. Forensic evidence collection requires the necessary knowledge, skills, and abilities to do so from the onset of patient care. ACFTA offers nurses and health care professionals across the state training to ensure trauma informed care for victims of violence which will ultimately promote justice for all victims in Alaska.C.E. Providers first complete the online portion of HeartCode BLS and then complete a hands-on skills session with an AHA BLS Instructor. events.description: This registration is for the live session. The on-demand session (Part 1) must be complete at least 2 days prior to the live session. This course will be December 10-12, 2024Each victim of violence deserves to be treated with dignity and respect, and to have evidence of the crimes committed against them preserved for future prosecution. Forensic evidence collection requires the necessary knowledge, skills, and abilities to do so from the onset of patient care. ACFTA offers nurses and health care professionals across the state training to ensure trauma informed care for victims of violence which will ultimately promote justice for all victims in Alaska.C.E. events.title: Live/Virtual Session (May 2024) - Alaska Comprehensive Forensic Training Academy (ACFTA)
events.description: This registration is for the live session. The online session (Part 1) must be complete at least 2 days prior to the live session. This course runs May 14-16th 2024, from 8am-5pm.Each victim of violence deserves to be treated with dignity and respect, and to have evidence of the crimes committed against them preserved for future prosecution. Unfortunately, many areas of the state lack the ability to document and collect medical evidence to help protect victims of crime. Forensic evidence collection requires the necessary knowledge, skills, and abilities to do so from the onset of patient care. ACFTA offers nurses and health care professionals across the state training to ensure trauma informed care for victims of violence which will ultimately promote justice for all victims in Alaska.C.E.

events.description: This registration is for the live session. The online session (Part 1) must be complete at least 2 days prior to the live session. This course runs December 12-14th 2023, from 8am-5pm.Each victim of violence deserves to be treated with dignity and respect, and to have evidence of the crimes committed against them preserved for future prosecution. Unfortunately, many areas of the state lack the ability to document and collect medical evidence to help protect victims of crime. Forensic evidence collection requires the necessary knowledge, skills, and abilities to do so from the onset of patient care. ACFTA offers nurses and health care professionals across the state training to ensure trauma informed care for victims of violence which will ultimately promote justice for all victims in Alaska

events.title: 2023 Alaska State of Reform Health Policy Conference
events.description: OverviewThe 2023 Alaska State of Reform Health Policy Conference will take place in-person at the Dena’ina Civic and Convention Center on October 11th, 2023!The State of Reform conference is focused on bridging the gap between health policy and political reality. Too often, those with good ideas for reforming healthcare don’t have a solid understanding of how the legislative process works. Those who understand politics don’t always comprehend the intricacies of the healthcare system. If you are interested in understanding what occurs at the nexus of healthcare and politics, then this is the conference for you!Join us in Anchorage on October 11th to discuss the most important healthcare activities taking place in Alaska. events.title: Creating Cultural Competence: Seeing Your Own Lens - Virtual Workshop - May 2023
events.description: On May 9th, from 2pm-4pm AKST, join the Alaska Humanities Forum for the Creating Cultural Competence virtual workshop.Participants will examine their own life experiences and analyze how their cultures influence their own definitions of health and wellness, and reflect on how and why they respond when working with people with cultures that define health and wellness differently.The Alaska Humanities Forum connects Alaskans through stories, ideas, and experiences that positively change lives and strengthen communities. The Forum's C3 (Creating Cultural Competence) workshops support aware, compassionate, and resilient cultural identity. This work provides a strong foundation upon which community connection and culturally responsive professional practices can be built.To register, click here!

events.title: Live/Virtual session - Alaska Comprehensive Forensic Training Academy (ACFTA) - 13 Dec 2022
events.description: This registration is for the live session. The online session (Part 1) must be complete at least 2 days prior to the live session. This course runs Dec 13-15th 2022, from 8am-5pm.Each victim of violence deserves to be treated with dignity and respect, and to have evidence of the crimes committed against them preserved for future prosecution. Unfortunately, many areas of the state lack the ability to document and collect medical evidence to help protect victims of crime. Forensic evidence collection requires the necessary knowledge, skills, and abilities to do so from the onset of patient care. ACFTA offers nurses and health care professionals across the state training to ensure trauma informed care for victims of violence which will ultimately promote justice for all victims in Alaska.C.E.

events.description: Join us as we utilize experiential learning techniques to introduce factors that contribute to building resilience. The goal of this 5-session series is twofold: to encourage resilience within the participant, and also to empower participants to build resilience in the students or clients they work with. Come prepared to interact with colleagues and have meaningful conversations related to resilience. Join us for one or all of the remaining four sessions.1. Resiliency Through Community (Completed: At All-COH Meeting)2. Planning for Resilience (October 14th 11am-1pm)3. Growing gratitude for Resilience (Nov 11th 11am-1pm)4.
